query:
=== Recent conversation (last 0 exchanges) ===
[tool: Agent(uni-pseudocode) → ]
[Assistant] Briefing captured — entry #3471 is a direct pattern for this exact scenario. Wave plan from the brief:

- **Wave 1** (parallel): `core-constants`, `hook-registration` — independent, no Rust cross-deps  
- **Wave 2** (parallel): `hook-dispatcher`, `observation-storage`, `friction-metrics` — all depend on Wave 1; `friction-metrics` consolidates both pre-post-differential-fix and tool-failure-rule (same file `friction.rs`)

Spawning Stage 3a — pseudocode and test plan in parallel.
=== End recent conversation ===

entries.title: ADR-004 col-023: Wave-Based Refactor Plan for HookType Blast Radius
Wave structure for multi-component features: parallel independent components first, sequential dependents second
ADR-008: Wave Ordering and Cross-Crate Synchronization
Batch bugfix swarm procedure: parallel investigators + parallel architects + single rust-dev + single tester
Long-wait parallel agent resumes with stale ADR context — tick-file agents must re-read architecture after 60+ min gaps
